symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden compelling urinary urgency, difficult to defer

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Urgency is the defining, cardinal symptom of overactive bladder syndrome.

symptoms.symptom_name: Urgency urinary incontinence (leak immediately preceded by urge)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Leakage preceded by a compelling urge is urgency incontinence, a core OAB feature.
Urge-preceded leakage is not the exertional (cough/laugh) pattern of stress incontinence.

symptoms.symptom_name: Increased daytime frequency (>15 voids/day) and near-hourly nocturia, small volumes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Frequency and nocturia with small voids accompany urgency in OAB.
Absence of dysuria makes bacterial cystitis less likely.
No visible haematuria lowers concern for a urothelial cancer; would mandate fast-track work-up if present.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal urinary stream, no hesitancy/straining, able to void (no retention)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal stream with preserved voiding argues strongly against obstruction/overflow retention.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of pain on bladder filling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Interstitial cystitis characteristically causes pain with bladder filling relieved by voiding; its absence argues against it.
Caffeine is a bladder irritant and diuretic that exacerbates urgency and frequency — a modifiable driver of the flare.
No fever or loin pain argues against upper tract infection / urosepsis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sterile point-of-care midstream urinalysis (negative nitrites, leukocyte esterase and blood)

investigation_details.expected_result: Negative for nitrites, leukocyte esterase and blood — a sterile specimen
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot safely exclude infection or haematuria; risk of mislabelling an infection as OAB or missing haematuria.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sterile dipstick makes bacterial cystitis very unlikely — but this is a clinician/point-of-care test the AI cannot perform remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, non-tender abdomen with no palpable bladder or suprapubic distension

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a palpable distended bladder argues against retention — a clinician examination finding, sealed and not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Detrusor overactivity on urodynamic filling phase (anticipated confirmatory finding)

investigation_details.expected_result: Involuntary detrusor contractions during filling, with no underlying metabolic pathology
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Not required for a straightforward first presentation; reserved for refractory or diagnostically uncertain cases.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Demonstrated detrusor overactivity confirms the mechanism, but is a specialist investigation never available in a telehealth consult.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Urgency-frequency syndrome without infective or obstructive features
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of cardinal urgency with frequency/nocturia and urge leakage, alongside a sterile urinalysis and preserved voiding, is a strong non-infective, non-obstructive OAB pattern.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Midstream urine dipstick and, if indicated, microscopy/culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line test to exclude infection and haematuria before attributing symptoms to OAB — requires in-person/pathology access the AI must arrange, not perform.
Excludes incomplete emptying/retention masquerading as frequency; part of the in-person GP assessment.
Quantifies frequency, functional capacity and fluid/caffeine intake to guide bladder retraining.
Not indicated for a straightforward first presentation; reserved for refractory or uncertain cases — the AI should not over-order it.
